label: Calf contusion without fracture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit a calf bruise.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a calf bruise. We did not find fracture, infection, open wound, or poor blood flow today.
home: Use ice wrapped in a cloth for short periods.
Return to normal activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not use medicine to push through worsening pain, numbness, or weakness.
return: New numbness, weakness, blue toes, pale toes, or severe worsening pain.
New deformity, inability to use the area, fever, spreading redness, or drainage.
Symptoms that worsen or do not improve as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated calf contusion.
No fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Fracture or dislocation concern, open wound, infection concern, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus bruise material supports contusion self-care, simple comfort measures, and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, infection signs, or concerning symptoms. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed calf contusion with fracture, wound, infection, neurovascular, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric, and specialist-directed pathways absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of calf contusion
